title: Deep Marching Cubes: Learning Explicit Surface Representations
problem: Learning-based 3D reconstruction methods often predict points, occupancy grids, or signed distance fields, then rely on a separate meshing step to obtain the actual surface.
Classical Marching Cubes is hard to train through: its interpolated vertex position has a singularity when adjacent signed distances match, and topology changes block useful gradients (**4a**).
Implicit targets such as TSDFs or occupancy grids require dense inside/outside supervision that is difficult or unreliable for sparse, noisy, incomplete, or non-watertight point clouds.
Auxiliary losses on intermediate representations do not directly constrain the final mesh geometry, so the learned model is not optimized for the surface that users care about.
solution: Deep Marching Cubes replaces signed-distance prediction with an explicit surface representation: each grid voxel predicts an occupancy probability and each cell edge receives a vertex displacement, separating topology from geometry (5c).

The Differentiable Marching Cubes Layer treats the eight corner occupancies of each cell as Bernoulli variables and induces a probability distribution over cell topologies (**4b**), restricted in practice to singly connected cases (5b).

A point-cloud encoder uses grid pooling followed by a 3D encoder-decoder with skip connections; the decoder splits into occupancy and displacement heads whose outputs feed the Differentiable Marching Cubes Layer (5d).

Training minimizes expected point-to-mesh error under the topology distribution (**4c**) together with occupancy, smoothness, and curvature terms (**4d**), allowing direct surface-based supervision from sparse points.

The occupancy prior anchors the scene boundary as free space and encourages an interior sub-volume to be occupied (**4e**), helping the model infer inside versus outside without watertight ground-truth meshes.
key_concepts: Explicit surface prediction in this paper means the network outputs local mesh parameters rather than an implicit field followed by post-processing; this is why the approach can optimize the final surface directly instead of a proxy representation (5a).

Topology distribution is the paper's differentiable substitute for the discrete Marching Cubes lookup: occupancies define probabilities over local face configurations, while vertex displacement field values define where the selected faces lie.

Separating topology from geometry avoids the zero-crossing singularity of classical Marching Cubes: topology can change through occupancy probabilities, and geometry can move through bounded edge displacements.

Grid pooling couples PointNet-style local feature extraction to the regular voxel lattice, preserving point-order invariance while producing features that a 3D convolutional decoder can process with skip connections (5d).

Surface-based supervision is central to the method: sparse observed points supervise distance to the predicted mesh, while priors and smoothness losses prevent trivial extra surfaces and propagate occupancy information away from observed cells.

variables.meaning: relative position of a mesh vertex along an edge between two cell corners
signed distance values at the two adjacent corners of the edge
probability of topology T in grid cell n
predicted occupancy probability at one of the eight cell corners
binary occupied/unoccupied state of corner m in topology T
offset indexing the eight corners of the cell
observed 3D points falling inside cell n
mesh induced in cell n by topology T and predicted vertex displacement field X
Euclidean point-to-mesh distance from point y to mesh M
weights balancing the mesh, occupancy, occupancy-smoothness, and curvature losses
loss encouraging neighboring occupancies to agree
expected loss encouraging connected neighboring faces to have similar normals
boundary voxels on the six faces of the scene cube, encouraged to be unoccupied
interior sub-volume encouraged to contain occupied voxels
predicted occupancy probability for voxel n
adaptive weight for the interior occupancy term

visuals.caption: Point prediction, implicit surface prediction, and explicit surface prediction
visuals.description: Compares output representations and shows that only the proposed explicit surface pipeline is end-to-end trainable for surface prediction.

visuals.caption: Marching Cubes mesh topology classes
visuals.description: Shows the 256 local topologies grouped by rotational symmetry and highlights the singly connected topologies retained by the method.

visuals.caption: Classical Marching Cubes versus Differentiable Marching Cubes representation
visuals.description: Contrasts signed-distance interpolation with the proposed occupancy and vertex displacement parameterization.

visuals.caption: Deep Marching Cubes network architecture
visuals.description: Shows grid pooling from point clouds, the 3D encoder-decoder with skip connections, and the two decoder heads for O and X.
